_comment: Illustrative RAMZA plan.json skeleton — Junction spec §7.5, schema `plan.v1` (Rynaro/Junction schemas/plan.v1.json). Mirrors the shape of Rynaro/Junction's examples/plan-trance/plan.json: {thread_id, tier, enforce, executor, steps[]}, each step {step_id, from{eidolon,version}, to{eidolon,version}, performative, edge_origin?, objective?, artifact?, model_tier_hint?, constraints?}. Fill every <placeholder>, then DELETE this `_comment` field before treating the file as a real plan.json — plan.v1 sets `additionalProperties:false` at every level (no vendor-extension escape hatch like ECL's `x_*` pattern), so a real `junction run <plan.json>` / `harness.run` will reject an unknown top-level key. Emitted at RAMZA's Assemble phase alongside the Markdown spec (see `templates/planning-artifact.md` 'Output Contract') when the consumer project runs Junction; a graceful no-op otherwise.
thread_id: <uuid-v7 — same thread_id as this spec's ECL envelope, so the trace correlates>

steps.objective: <One sentence: what ATLAS should scout before RAMZA plans, e.g. 'Scout the auth module for MFA extension points.'>

artifact.path: <relative path to the scouting prompt, e.g. input/scout-prompt.md>

steps.objective: <One sentence: refine ATLAS's scout report into a decision-ready RAMZA spec, e.g. 'Produce the RS->S->P->E->C->T->A spec for TOTP-based MFA.'>
<One sentence: implement RAMZA's frozen, verified spec, e.g. 'Implement the MFA spec per its EARS acceptance criteria.'>
